claimText: Heavy metals are characterized by a high atomic weight ranging from 63.5 to 200.6 and a density greater than 4000 kg/m3.
evidenceExcerpt: Characteristics: High atomic weight ranging from 63.5 to 200.6 and density >4000 kg/m3.
examUse: Statement-based question on the physical properties defining heavy metals.

claimText: Heavy metals and metalloids are nondegradable and bioaccumulate in soils over decadal timescales.
evidenceExcerpt: They are nondegradable and bioaccumulate in soils over decadal timescales.
examUse: Statement-based question on environmental persistence and bioaccumulation.

claimText: Exposure to Cadmium can cause kidney damage, bone damage, and lung cancer.
evidenceExcerpt: Cadmium exposure can cause kidney and bone damage, and lung cancer.
examUse: Correct-pair question matching heavy metals to their specific health impacts.

claimText: Lead exposure causes developmental and neurobehavioral effects on fetuses.
evidenceExcerpt: Lead exposure causes developmental and neurobehavioral effects on fetuses.
examUse: Correct-pair question matching heavy metals to their specific health impacts.

claimText: Inorganic arsenic is highly toxic and can cause skin lesions, cardiovascular disease, and diabetes.
evidenceExcerpt: Arsenic is highly toxic in its inorganic form and can cause skin lesions, cardiovascular disease, and diabetes.
examUse: Correct-pair question matching heavy metals to their specific health impacts.
distractorHooks: Organic arsenic is the highly toxic form
